frame_analyses.description: Needle loaded onto the driver, right hand holding needle driver, left hand holding the tissue.
Needle passing through the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate bite placement with good needle control.

frame_analyses.description: Suture being pulled through the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth suture traction with minimal excess tension.

frame_analyses.description: First throw initiated, preparing for the surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw being set up; right hand maintaining tension.

frame_analyses.description: Completion of the first throw with a surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Secure and proper double throw observed.
Hands are switched to alternate control.
Knot appears well secured, hand alternation is evident.

frame_analyses.description: Starting the third throw to finalize the knot.
Final knot assessed, ends of the suture not yet cut.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot secure with good tissue approximation.

knot_assessments.notes: Final knot is stable with good tension.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gaps, good slit closure.

technique_summary: The procedure was carried out with good control and consistent knot securing. No significant gaps or knot slippage was noted.
improvement_suggestions: Increase speed of completion without sacrificing accuracy.
